message:
test(sandbox_isolation): replace .unwrap_err() with explicit match arms on create_store rejection paths

The four preopen-rejection assertions in
`sandbox_isolation_conformance.rs` (symlinked readonly, symlinked
writable, file readonly, file writable) were structured as:

    let err = runtime.create_store().unwrap_err().to_string();

That compiles and does the right thing in the passing case, but it
collapses to a single opaque "called `.unwrap_err()` on an `Ok`
value" panic if the underlying WASI runtime ever silently starts
accepting a bad preopen — which is the exact regression class these
conformance tests exist to catch. Reading the panic message in a CI
log does not make it obvious *which* of the four assertions regressed
or *what* the test was actually checking.

This commit replaces each of those four sites with an explicit
match arm:

    let err = match runtime.create_store() {
        Ok(_) => panic!("expected <description> preopen to be rejected"),
        Err(err) => err.to_string(),
    };

Each panic message names the scenario:

- `"expected readonly symlinked preopen to be rejected"`
- `"expected writable symlinked preopen to be rejected"`
- `"expected readonly file preopen to be rejected"`
- `"expected writable file preopen to be rejected"`

so a failing CI run points at the specific rejection path that
regressed without having to bisect four visually-identical test
bodies.

Wire-level behaviour unchanged: passing tests still pass, failing
tests still fail, the only difference is that a failure carries a
grepable description instead of a generic `unwrap_err` panic
message.
